concerns.text: Task T004 enforces FR-001 ('exactly 5 datasets') despite the plan.md and spec deviation section explicitly documenting a reduction to 3 verified datasets. This task silently weakens the approved plan's feasibility constraint and violates the 'Spec Deviation & Mitigation' agreement, creating a direct conflict where the implementation will fail the plan's stated scope.

Task T007 states it 'Requires T008 schema definition,' yet T008 is listed as a parallel [P] task preceding T007 in the phase list. This creates a logical execution order conflict where a blocking dependency is assigned to a parallel task, violating the deterministic execution flow required for the Monte Carlo loop (FR-004).

Task T030 mandates injecting a disclaimer into 'every result JSON file,' but the naming conventions in T015 and T023 define separate files for Null and Alt conditions. The task lacks a specific file-discovery mechanism (e.g., glob pattern or directory scan) to ensure *all* generated variants (including intermediate or partial runs) are captured, risking a violation of FR-007 if any file is missed.

Task T005 specifies handling edge cases by 'logging warnings and skipping configurations,' but does not explicitly mandate the creation of a 'skipped_configurations.log' or a structured record in the results directory. This risks violating Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) and Principle IV (Single Source of Truth) by failing to provide a traceable record of why specific dataset-size combinations were excluded from the final analysis.
